title: Hydrocotyle solandra
taxonomy: Hydrocotyle solandra is classified within the family Araliaceae. This classification places it among the ginseng and ivy family. Within the genus Hydrocotyle, it belongs to a group of aquatic plants commonly known as pennyworts. The exact classification within the family Araliaceae can be further broken down into the order Apiales. Hydrocotyle solandra's classification is a key part of understanding its unique characteristics and growth habits.

intro: Hydrocotyle solandra, also known as Pennywort or Brazilian Pennywort, is a perennial aquatic plant native to South America, belonging to the family Araliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to improve cognitive function, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, thus providing potential health benefits for the brain and nervous system. Hydrocotyle solandra contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, capsules, and extracts, often used to support cognitive function and promote overall well-being.
